steps.title: Trace the image
steps.content: The underlined line observes wind shaking seed-pods. The rest of the poem turns that natural image into a description of the speaker's thoughts as spent, feverish, torn, and scattered like seeds. The outside movement becomes the model for the speaker's inner state, so the line launches the poem's governing comparison.

steps.title: Avoid cause and contrast
steps.content: A treats the wind as the cause of the speaker's misgivings, but the poem uses it as an image. B invents consistency in nature and a contrast with emotions, while the poem links the natural scene and the thoughts. D adds a claim about common self-evaluation, not a separate natural event or ordinary detail. The answer is C.
